args.description: Optional arguments to pass to the agent command

args-read-only.description: Optional arguments to pass only when the agent is executed in read-only mode. When present, these are preferred over `args` for read-only runs.

args-write.description: Optional arguments to pass only when the agent is executed with write permissions. When present, these are preferred over `args` for write runs.

command.description: Command to execute the agent (e.g., "claude", "gemini"). If omitted, defaults to the agent `name` during config load.

env.description: Optional environment variables for the agent

instructions.description: Optional per-agent instructions. When set, these are prepended to the prompt provided to the agent whenever it runs.

read-only.description: Whether this agent can only run in read-only mode

AskForApproval.description: Determines the conditions under which the user is consulted to approve running the command proposed by Codex.

oneOf.description: Under this policy, only "known safe" commands—as determined by `is_safe_command()`—that **only read files** are auto‑approved. Everything else will ask the user to approve.

DEPRECATED: *All* commands are auto‑approved, but they are expected to run inside a sandbox where network access is disabled and writes are confined to a specific set of paths. If the command fails, it will be escalated to the user to approve execution without a sandbox. Prefer `OnRequest` for interactive runs or `Never` for non-interactive runs.
The model decides when to ask the user for approval.

Fine-grained rejection controls for approval prompts.

When a field is `true`, prompts of that category are automatically rejected instead of shown to the user.
Never ask the user to approve commands. Failures are immediately returned to the model, and never escalated to the user for approval.

AuthCredentialsStoreMode.description: Determine where Code should store CLI auth credentials (the `auth.json` payload).

oneOf.description: Persist credentials in `CODE_HOME/auth.json`.
Persist credentials in an OS-specific keyring service. Fail if unavailable.
Use keyring when available; otherwise, fall back to a file in `CODE_HOME`.
Store credentials in memory only for the current process.
`OpenAI` API key provided by the caller and stored by Codex.
`ChatGPT` OAuth managed by Codex (tokens persisted and refreshed by Codex).

[UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE.

`ChatGPT` auth tokens are supplied by an external host app and are only stored in memory. Token refresh must be handled by the external host app.

AutoDriveSettings.description: Auto Drive behavioral defaults persisted via `config.toml`.

coordinator_routing.description: Enable coordinator routing of user prompts during Auto Drive turns.

coordinator_turn_cap.description: Maximum number of coordinator turns before stopping the session (0 = unlimited).

model.description: Model used for the Auto Drive coordinator and follow-on turns.

model_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort applied to the Auto Drive model.

model_routing_enabled.description: Allow the coordinator to select the CLI model and reasoning effort per turn.

default.description: Hard planning and complex problem solving
Fast implementation loops and failing-test iteration

model_routing_entries.description: Per-model routing entries used by coordinator-driven CLI model routing.

title.description: Optional title shown in the TUI header and intro animation. When unset or empty, Codex falls back to its built-in title.

BrowserConfig.description: Browser configuration for integrated screenshot capabilities.

CachedTerminalBackground.description: Collection of settings that are specific to the TUI.

ClientTools.description: MCP tool identifiers that the client exposes to the agent.

dangerous_rules.description: Ruleset used to decide whether a command is dangerous.

safe_rules.description: Ruleset used to decide whether a command is "known safe".

dangerous_rules.description: Ruleset used to decide whether a command is dangerous.

safe_rules.description: Ruleset used to decide whether a command is "known safe".

ConfigProfile.description: Collection of common configuration options that a user can define as a unit in `config.toml`.

api_key_fallback_on_all_accounts_limited.description: When true, fall back to an API key account only if every connected `ChatGPT` account is rate/usage limited.

auto_switch_accounts_on_rate_limit.description: When true, automatically switch to another connected account when the current account hits a rate/usage limit.

model_provider.description: The key in the `model_providers` map identifying the [`ModelProviderInfo`] to use.

ConfirmGuardConfig.description: Configuration for commands that require an explicit `confirm:` prefix.

patterns.description: List of regex patterns applied to the raw command (joined argv or shell script).

message.description: Optional custom guidance text surfaced when the guard triggers.

regex.description: ECMA-style regular expression matched against the command string.

label.description: Optional human-readable label to display in the UI

ExecLimitsToml.description:
Execution limits for tool-spawned processes.

These are enforced via Linux cgroup v2 when available. On other platforms, the settings are currently best-effort and primarily affect what we attach to the environment/sandbox runner.

memory_max_mb.description:
Memory limit applied via cgroup `memory.max` (mebibytes).

- `"auto"`: dynamic default - `"disabled"`: no memory cap - `<int>`: explicit cap

pids_max.description:
Process limit applied via cgroup `pids.max`.

- `"auto"`: dynamic default - `"disabled"`: no process cap - `<int>`: explicit cap

actionlint_on_patch.description: When true, run `actionlint` on modified workflows during `apply_patch`.

actionlint_path.description: Optional explicit executable path for actionlint.

actionlint_strict.description: Treat actionlint findings as blocking when composing approval text.

check_workflows_on_push.description: When true, Codex watches for GitHub Actions workflow runs after a successful `git push` and reports failures as background messages. Enabled by default; can be disabled via `~/.code/config.toml` under `[github]` with `check_workflows_on_push = false`.

bottom_line_text.description: Optional custom text for the secondary header line. When unset and `show_bottom_line = true`, the line is rendered empty.

hover_style.description: Hover styling for interactive header segments (model/shell/reasoning).

show_bottom_line.description: Show the optional secondary line under the primary header line.

top_line_text.description: Optional custom text for the primary header line. When unset, Codex renders its default dynamic status content.

HeaderHoverStyle.description: Hover styling options for interactive segments in the TUI header.

oneOf.description: Highlight interactive segments with a background fill (and emphasis).
Only underline interactive segments on hover.
Disable hover styling for interactive segments.

HighlightConfig.description:
Configuration for syntax highlighting in Markdown code blocks.

`theme` accepts the following values: - "auto" (default): choose a sensible built-in syntect theme based on whether the current UI theme is light or dark. - "<name>": use a specific syntect theme by name from the default `ThemeSet`.

theme.description: Theme selection preference (see docstring for accepted values)

History.description: Settings that govern if and what will be written to `~/.code/history.jsonl` (Code still reads legacy `~/.codex/history.jsonl`).

persistence.description: If true, history entries will not be written to disk.

oneOf.description: Save all history entries to disk.
Do not write history to disk.

IconMode.description: Icon display mode controlling which glyph tier is rendered.

oneOf.description: `NerdFont` PUA glyphs (requires a NerdFont-patched terminal font).
Standard Unicode symbols (the default — works in any modern terminal).
Pure ASCII fallbacks (for minimal/legacy terminals).

anyOf.description: Override all tiers with the same string.

IconOverrideValue.description:
Per-icon override — either a single string for all tiers, or per-tier strings.

```toml [tui.icons] checkbox_on = "✅"             # override all tiers

[tui.icons.gutter_user]        # override individual tiers ascii = ">" unicode = "›" ```

LifecycleHooksToml.description:
Settings for the upstream-compatible `hooks.json` lifecycle hooks engine.

These values are persisted under `[lifecycle_hooks]` in `config.toml`.

enabled.description: When `Some(false)`, disables the `hooks.json` lifecycle hook engine. When omitted, hooks are enabled.

shell_args.description: Additional args passed to the hook shell program.

shell_program.description: Optional explicit shell program to use when running hook commands. When omitted, Code derives a shell from the session's detected shell.

LimitsLayoutMode.description: Available layout modes for the limits settings panel.

layout_mode.description: Preferred layout mode for the limits settings panel.

McpToolId.description: Identifier for a client-hosted MCP tool.

generate_memories.description: Enable automatic memory extraction from session history.

max_raw_memories_for_consolidation.description: Maximum number of recent raw memories retained for consolidation.

max_rollout_age_days.description: Maximum age (days) of threads eligible for memory extraction.

max_rollouts_per_startup.description: Maximum number of rollout candidates processed per startup pass.

min_rollout_idle_hours.description: Minimum idle hours between last thread activity and memory extraction.

no_memories_if_mcp_or_web_search.description: Skip memory extraction for sessions that invoke MCP tools or web search.

use_memories.description: Inject extracted memories into LLM prompts.

admin_url.description: Admin API listener address (must be loopback unless explicitly overridden).

allow_local_binding.description: Allow binding loopback listeners from within mediated sessions.

allow_unix_sockets.description: Allow proxying requests to these unix sockets (macOS only).

allow_upstream_proxy.description: Allow honoring upstream proxy environment variables (defense-in-depth is still enforced by the managed proxy policy).

allowed_domains.description: Allow-list of domain globs. When empty, all hosts are treated as not allowed and may trigger the interactive approval flow (when enabled).

dangerously_allow_non_loopback_admin.description: DANGEROUS: allow binding the admin listener on a non-loopback address.

dangerously_allow_non_loopback_proxy.description: DANGEROUS: allow binding the proxy listener on a non-loopback address.

denied_domains.description: Deny-list of domain globs. Denies always win over allows.

enable_socks5_udp.description: Enable SOCKS5 UDP (only used when SOCKS5 is enabled).

enabled.description: Enable managed network proxy mediation for sandboxed command execution.

mode.description: Network policy mode (method restrictions and tunneling behavior).

proxy_url.description: HTTP proxy listener address (must be loopback unless explicitly overridden).

socks_url.description: SOCKS5 listener address (must be loopback unless explicitly overridden).

Notice.description: User acknowledgements for in-product notices (distinct from notifications).

OAuthCredentialsStoreMode.description: Determine where Code should store and read MCP OAuth credentials.

oneOf.description: `Keyring` when available; otherwise, `File`.
`CODE_HOME/.credentials.json`
Keyring when available, otherwise fail.
Minimal reasoning. Accepts legacy value "none" for backwards compatibility.

ReasoningSummary.description: A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process. See <https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning?api-mode=responses#reasoning-summaries>

oneOf.description: Option to disable reasoning summaries.

request_permissions.description: Reject approval prompts related to built-in permission requests.

rules.description: Reject prompts triggered by execpolicy `prompt` rules.

sandbox_approval.description: Reject approval prompts related to sandbox escalation.

skill_approval.description: Reject approval prompts triggered by skill script execution.

allow_git_writes.description: When true, do not protect the top-level `.git` folder under a writable root. Defaults to true (historical behavior allows Git writes).

oneOf.description: Legacy compatibility value for older local config files.

open_mode.description: Which settings UI to open by default.

overlay_min_width.description: Minimum terminal width (columns) to prefer the overlay UI when `open_mode = "auto"`.

oneOf.description: Choose between the overlay and bottom-pane settings UIs based on the current terminal width.
Always open the full-screen settings overlay ("big menu").
Always open the bottom-pane settings UI ("bottom menu") when possible.

dangerous_command_detection.description: Optional override for dangerous-command detection in command safety. When omitted, shell-specific defaults are used.

oneOf.description: "Core" environment variables for the platform. On UNIX, this would include HOME, LOGNAME, PATH, SHELL, and USER, among others.
Inherits the full environment from the parent process.
Do not inherit any environment variables from the parent process.

ShellEnvironmentPolicyToml.description: Policy for building the `env` when spawning a process via either the `shell` or `local_shell` tool.

command_safety.description: Command-safety overrides applied when this style profile is active.

dangerous_command_detection.description: Optional override for dangerous-command detection when this style is active. This setting takes precedence over shell-level configuration.

disabled_skills.description: Optional list of skill names to disable when this style is active. These entries override `skills` and any skills loaded from roots.

mcp_servers.description: MCP server include/exclude filters applied when this style is active.

prepend_developer_messages.description: Additional style-specific developer messages prepended each turn.

references.description: Additional style-specific reference files to load into model context. Relative paths are resolved against the session cwd.

skill_roots.description: Additional skill root directories to scan when this style is active. Relative paths are resolved against the session cwd.

skills.description: Optional list of skill names to inject when this style is active. When non-empty, only these skill names are included.

summary.description: Optional user-defined summary describing what this style profile does. Used for UI affordances like shell profile pickers and settings.

custom.description: Custom spinner definitions saved by the user

name.description: Name of the spinner to use. Accepts one of the names from sindresorhus/cli-spinners (kebab-case), or custom names supported by Codex. Defaults to "diamond".

StreamConfig.description: Streaming behavior configuration for the TUI.

answer_header_immediate.description: Emit the Answer header immediately when a stream begins (before first newline).

commit_tick_ms.description: Commit animation pacing in milliseconds (lines per `CommitTick`). If unset, defaults to 50ms; in responsive profile, defaults to 30ms.

relax_code_holdback.description: Relax code hold-back: allow committing inside an open fenced code block except the very last partial line.

relax_list_holdback.description: Relax list hold-back: allow list lines with content; only withhold bare markers.

responsive.description: Convenience switch enabling a snappier preset for the above values. Explicit values above still take precedence if set.

show_answer_ellipsis.description: Show an ellipsis placeholder in the Answer body while waiting for first text.

soft_commit_chars.description: Soft-commit when this many chars have streamed without a newline. If unset, disabled; in responsive profile, defaults to 160 chars.

soft_commit_timeout_ms.description: Soft-commit timeout (ms) when no newline arrives; commits partial content. If unset, disabled; in responsive profile, defaults to 400ms.

SubagentCommandConfig.description: Configuration for a subagent slash command (e.g., plan/solve/code or custom)

agent-instructions.description: Extra instructions that the orchestrator should append to the prompt given to each launched agent.

agents.description: Agent names to enable for this command. If empty, falls back to enabled agents from `[[agents]]`, or built-in defaults.

name.description: Name of the command (e.g., "plan", "solve", "code", or custom)

orchestrator-instructions.description: Extra instructions to append to the orchestrator (Code) prompt.

read-only.description: Whether agents launched for this command should run in read-only mode Defaults: plan/solve=true, code=false (applied if not specified here)

SubagentsToml.description: Top-level subagents section containing a list of commands.

max-depth.description: Maximum nesting depth for agent-spawned agent runs. `1` allows root sessions to spawn agents, but blocks further nesting.

TextVerbosity.description: Text verbosity level for `OpenAI` API responses. Controls the level of detail in the model's text responses.

ThemeColors.description: Theme colors that can be customized

is_dark.description: Optional hint whether the custom theme targets a dark background. When present and `name == Custom`, the UI can show "Dark - <label>" or "Light - <label>" in lists.

label.description: Optional display name when using a custom theme generated by the user. Not used for built-in themes. If `name == Custom` and this is set, the UI may display it in place of the generic "Custom" label.

name.description: Name of the predefined theme to use

js_repl.description: Enable the optional `js_repl` tool (off by default).

js_repl_node_module_dirs.description:
Extra directories to search for packages when using the Node runtime.

Each entry may be either: - a `node_modules` directory, or - a project root containing a `node_modules` directory.

These paths are passed to the `js_repl` kernel via `CODEX_JS_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS` and are used only for packages-only (bare) imports.

js_repl_runtime_args.description: Additional arguments passed to the `js_repl` runtime process.

js_repl_runtime_path.description: Optional explicit path to the runtime executable used by `js_repl`. When omitted, the runtime is resolved from PATH (for example `node`).

view_image.description: Enable the `image_view` tool that lets the agent attach local images.

web_search_allowed_domains.description:
Optional allow-list of domains used by the Responses API `web_search` tool. Example:

[tools] `web_search` = true `web_search_allowed_domains` = ["openai.com", "arxiv.org"]

web_search_external.description: Controls `external_web_access` for the `web_search` tool payload. Defaults to true when omitted.

alternate_screen.description: Whether to use the terminal's Alternate Screen (full-screen) mode. When false, Codex renders nothing and leaves the standard terminal buffer visible; users can toggle back to Alternate Screen at runtime with Ctrl+T. Defaults to true.

auto_drive.description: Auto Drive behavioral defaults (legacy location; prefer top-level `[auto_drive]`).

auto_review_enabled.description: Run a background `/review` after turns that modify code.

branding.description: Branding text shown in the top header and intro animation.

cached_terminal_background.description: Cached autodetect result so we can skip probing the terminal repeatedly.

header.description: Header display options for the top status area.

highlight.description: Syntax highlighting configuration (Markdown fenced code blocks)

hotkeys.description: Keyboard shortcut preferences (for status line quick actions).

icon_mode.description: Icon display mode: `nerd_fonts`, `unicode`, or `ascii`. Replaces the legacy `nerd_fonts` boolean.

icons.description: Per-icon overrides.  Keys match the accessor function names in `tui/src/icons.rs` (e.g. `gutter_user`, `bullet`, `arrow_left`). A string value overrides all tiers; a table with `nerd`, `unicode`, and/or `ascii` keys overrides individual tiers.

limits.description: Rate-limit panel layout preferences for the settings overlay.

nerd_fonts.description: **Deprecated** — use `icon_mode` instead. Legacy boolean: `true` maps to `icon_mode = "nerd_fonts"`.

notifications.description: Enable desktop notifications from the TUI when the terminal is unfocused. Defaults to `false`.

review_auto_resolve.description: Remember whether Auto Resolve is enabled for `/review` flows.

settings_menu.description: Routing preferences for the Settings UI (overlay vs bottom pane).

shell_presets.description: Optional user-defined shell presets for the shell selector UI.

shell_presets_file.description: Optional path to a TOML file containing additional shell presets. Relative paths are resolved against the session cwd.

show_reasoning.description: Whether to show reasoning content expanded by default (can be toggled with Ctrl+R/T)

status_line_bottom.description:
Ordered list of status line item identifiers for the bottom header line.

Used when `tui.header.show_bottom_line = true` and `tui.header.bottom_line_text` is unset.

status_line_primary.description: Which status line lane `/statusline` opens by default.

status_line_top.description:
Ordered list of status line item identifiers for the top header line.

When set, the built-in top-line renderer uses these items (in order) instead of the legacy fixed layout. Custom templates in `tui.header.top_line_text` still take precedence when present.

upgrade_command.description:
Optional custom upgrade command for `/update` and the Upgrade settings UI.

When set, Code uses this command in place of installer inference (npm/bun/Homebrew). This is the intended way to support other installation methods (for example, `cargo` or `nix`) without adding brittle heuristics.

TuiHotkey.description: Hotkey binding. Examples: "f2", "ctrl+h", "alt+h", "ctrl+alt+h", "disabled". Some actions also accept "legacy". Note: F1 is reserved for the Help overlay.

exec_output_fold.description:
Keyboard shortcut for toggling output folding on the latest exec cell.

Defaults to the legacy mapping (`[` while the composer is empty).

js_repl_code_fold.description:
Keyboard shortcut for toggling code folding on the latest JS REPL cell.

Defaults to the legacy mapping (`\\` while the composer is empty).

jump_to_latest_child_call.description:
Keyboard shortcut for jumping to the latest tool call spawned by the latest JS REPL cell.

Defaults to the legacy mapping (`}` while the composer is empty).

jump_to_parent_call.description:
Keyboard shortcut for jumping to the parent of the latest nested tool call.

Defaults to the legacy mapping (`]` while the composer is empty).

model_selector.description: Keyboard shortcut for opening the model selector (mirrors clicking the status line).

network_settings.description: Keyboard shortcut for opening Settings -> Network (mirrors clicking the status line).

reasoning_effort.description: Keyboard shortcut for cycling reasoning effort (mirrors clicking the status line).

shell_selector.description: Keyboard shortcut for opening the shell selector (mirrors clicking the status line).

termux.description: Optional overrides under `[tui.hotkeys.termux]` (applied on top of android).

TuiHotkeysOverrides.description:
Optional per-platform overrides for hotkey preferences.

Nested under `[tui.hotkeys.<platform>]`.

exec_output_fold.description: Keyboard shortcut for toggling exec output folding (history shortcut).

js_repl_code_fold.description: Keyboard shortcut for toggling JS REPL code folding (history shortcut).

jump_to_latest_child_call.description: Keyboard shortcut for jumping to the latest child tool call (history shortcut).

jump_to_parent_call.description: Keyboard shortcut for jumping to the parent tool call (history shortcut).

oneOf.description: Option to disable the URI-based file opener.

groups.description: Group toggles that control which classes of validation run.

patch_harness.description: Legacy master toggle for the validation harness (kept for config compatibility). `run_patch_harness` now relies solely on the functional/stylistic group toggles.

timeout_seconds.description: Timeout (seconds) for each external tool invocation.

tools_allowlist.description: Optional allowlist restricting which external tools may run.

stylistic.description: Stylistic checks enforce formatting and best practices.

description: Base config deserialized from ~/.code/config.toml (legacy ~/.codex/config.toml is still read).

api_key_fallback_on_all_accounts_limited.description: When true, fall back to an API key account only if every connected `ChatGPT` account is rate/usage limited.

approval_policy.description: Default approval policy for executing commands.

auto_drive_use_chat_model.description: If true, Auto Drive inherits the chat model instead of a dedicated override.

auto_review_model.description: Auto Review model override used for background reviews.

auto_review_model_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override used for the Auto Review model.

auto_review_resolve_model.description: Resolve model override used during Auto Review follow-ups.

auto_review_resolve_model_reasoning_effo.description: Reasoning effort override used for the Auto Review resolve model.

auto_review_resolve_use_chat_model.description: Inherit chat model for Auto Review resolve flows when true.

auto_review_use_chat_model.description: Inherit chat model for Auto Review when true.

auto_switch_accounts_on_rate_limit.description: When true, automatically switch to another connected account when the current account hits a rate/usage limit.

auto_upgrade_enabled.description: Enable silent upgrades during startup when a newer release is available.

browser.description: Browser configuration for integrated screenshot capabilities.

chatgpt_base_url.description: Base URL for requests to `ChatGPT` (as opposed to the `OpenAI` API).

cli_auth_credentials_store.description:
Preferred backend for storing CLI auth credentials.

- `file` (default): Use `CODE_HOME/auth.json`. - `keyring`: Use an OS-specific keyring service. - `auto`: Use the keyring when available; otherwise fall back to `file`. - `ephemeral`: Store credentials in memory only for the current process.

compact_prompt_file.description: Path to a file whose contents should replace the compaction prompt template.

compact_prompt_override.description: Optional override string for the compaction prompt.

disable_paste_burst.description: When true, disables burst-paste detection for typed input entirely. All characters are inserted as they are received, and no buffering or placeholder replacement will occur for fast keypress bursts.

disable_response_storage.description: Disable server-side response storage (sends the full conversation context with every request). Currently necessary for `OpenAI` customers who have opted into Zero Data Retention (ZDR).

dynamic_tools.description: Dynamic tool specifications injected by the client.

exec_limits.description:
Limits applied to tool-spawned processes (Linux cgroup v2 when available).

Persisted under `[exec_limits]` in `config.toml`.

experimental_client_tools.description: Optional ACP client tool identifiers supplied by the host IDE.

experimental_exec_server_url.description: Experimental / do not use. Overrides the URL used when connecting to a remote exec server.

experimental_instructions_file.description: Experimental path to a file whose contents replace the built-in `BASE_INSTRUCTIONS`.

experimental_resume.description: Experimental path to a rollout file to resume from.

experimental_spawn_exec_server.description: Experimental / do not use. When set, spawns a local exec-server subprocess and routes supported operations through it.

file_opener.description: Optional URI-based file opener. If set, citations to files in the model output will be hyperlinked using the specified URI scheme.

hide_agent_reasoning.description: When set to `true`, `AgentReasoning` events will be hidden from the UI/output. Defaults to `false`.

history.description: Settings that govern if and what will be written to `~/.code/history.jsonl` (Code still reads legacy `~/.codex/history.jsonl`).

main_execve_wrapper_exe.description:
Optional override path to the `codex-execve-wrapper` helper binary.

When unset, Code will try to auto-discover the wrapper at runtime (as a sibling of the main executable, then via `PATH`).

mcp_oauth_callback_port.description: Optional fixed port to use for the local HTTP callback server used during MCP OAuth login.

mcp_oauth_credentials_store.description:
Preferred store for MCP OAuth credentials (used by streamable HTTP MCP servers).

- `auto` (default): keyring if available, otherwise file. - `file`: `CODE_HOME/.credentials.json` - `keyring`: OS keyring service (fails if unavailable).

mcp_servers.description: Definition for MCP servers that Codex can reach out to for tool calls.

model_auto_compact_token_limit.description: Token usage threshold triggering auto-compaction of conversation history.

model_context_window.description: Size of the context window for the model, in tokens.

model_provider.description: Provider to use from the `model_providers` map.

model_supports_reasoning_summaries.description: Override to force-enable reasoning summaries for the configured model.

network.description: Optional managed network proxy configuration used to mediate outbound network access from sandboxed command execution.

notice.description: Stored acknowledgement flags for in-product notices.

notify.description: Optional external command to spawn for end-user notifications.

planning_model.description: Planning model override used when in Read Only (Plan Mode).

planning_model_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override used for the planning model.

planning_use_chat_model.description: Inherit chat model for planning mode when true.

preferred_auth_method.description: If set to `true`, the API key will be signed with the `originator` header.

profile.description: Profile to use from the `profiles` map.

profiles.description: Named profiles to facilitate switching between different configurations.

project_doc_fallback_filenames.description: Ordered list of fallback filenames to look for when AGENTS.md is missing.

project_doc_max_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes to include from an AGENTS.md project doc file.

responses_originator_header_internal_ove.description: The value for the `originator` header included with Responses API requests.

review_model.description: Review model override used by the `/review` feature.

review_model_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override used for the review model.

review_resolve_model.description: Resolve model override used during auto-resolve for `/review`.

review_resolve_model_reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort override used for the resolve model.

review_resolve_use_chat_model.description: Inherit chat model for resolve flows when true.

review_use_chat_model.description: Inherit chat model for review flows when true.

sandbox_workspace_write.description: Sandbox configuration to apply if `sandbox` is `WorkspaceWrite`.

shell.description: Shell configuration for command execution. If not set, the user's default shell is detected automatically.

shell_style_profiles.description: Optional shell-style resource profiles keyed by style slug.

show_raw_agent_reasoning.description: When set to `true`, `AgentReasoningRawContentEvent` events will be shown in the UI/output. Defaults to `false`.

subagents.description: Configuration for subagent commands (built-ins and custom).

tool_output_max_bytes.description: Maximum number of bytes of tool output to include in a model request.

tools.description: Nested tools section for feature toggles

tui.description: Collection of settings that are specific to the TUI.

zsh_path.description:
Absolute path to a patched `zsh` binary that supports `EXEC_WRAPPER`.

Required for the Unix-only zsh-fork shell escalation backend (`features.shell_zsh_fork=true`).

title: ConfigToml
